title: Fragaria supina
taxonomy: Fragaria supina is a species of strawberry. It belongs to the family Rosaceae, commonly known as the rose family. The genus Fragaria is a group of flowering plants that also includes other strawberry species. Fragaria supina is native to Asia and is characterized by its small white flowers and edible fruit. This species is closely related to other strawberry species within the Fragaria genus.
uses: Fragaria supina, also known as woodland strawberry, has been used in traditional medicine for its potential benefits. The plant's leaves, flowers, and roots have been used to treat a variety of ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues. In some cultures, Fragaria supina is used as an antiseptic and anti-inflammatory agent to soothe skin irritations and wounds. The plant's extracts have also been studied for their antioxidant properties and potential to reduce oxidative stress. However, more research is needed to confirm the efficacy and safety of using Fragaria supina for medicinal purposes.
benefits: Fragaria supina, also known as beach strawberry or low sweet strawberry, is a species of strawberry native to the coastal regions of East Asia and the Russian Far East. This plant is rich in antioxidants, vitamins, and minerals, which contribute to its potential health benefits. The fruit of Fragaria supina has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health issues, including inflammation and digestive problems. The plant's leaves and roots are also believed to have anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties. Further research is needed to fully understand the health benefits of Fragaria supina.
properties: Fragaria supina, commonly known as the creeping wood sorrel, has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its potential therapeutic properties. The plant's leaves are rich in antioxidants and flavonoids, which may contribute to its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic effects. Research suggests that Fragaria supina may have antimicrobial properties, making it a potential natural remedy for wound healing and skin infections. The plant's leaves may also have diuretic properties, which could aid in treating urinary tract infections and other kidney-related issues. However, more studies are needed to fully understand the plant's therapeutic potential and its efficacy in treating various health conditions.
constituents: Fragaria supina, also known as the low sweet vetch or sweet vetch, is a species of flowering plant in the rose family. Fragaria supina contains a variety of active constituents, including flavonoids, phenolic acids, and anthocyanins, which contribute to its medicinal properties.
